places.name: Tirupati — Venkateswara
places.modernInfo: Richest temple in the world. 50,000+ pilgrims daily. Venkateswara is Vishnu's form for Kali Yuga.

scenes.title: Vishnu descends to Kali Yuga
scenes.description: When Kali Yuga began, Vishnu chose to live on the Venkata hill to be accessible to all devotees regardless of caste or status. He is the only deity who stays on Earth during Kali Yuga.

scenes.title: Padmavathi's wedding loan
scenes.description: Vishnu borrowed money from Kubera to marry Padmavathi. He is still repaying this loan — every offering made by pilgrims goes toward this divine debt. This is why Tirupati is the wealthiest temple.

places.name: Puri — Jagannath
places.modernInfo: One of 4 Char Dham. Rath Yatra draws 1 million+ pilgrims. Prasad is cooked in world's largest temple kitchen.

scenes.title: Vishnu becomes Jagannath
scenes.description: Vishnu took the form of Jagannath — Lord of the Universe — at Puri. Unusually, he is depicted without hands or feet, symbolising that the divine is beyond form.

scenes.title: Rath Yatra
scenes.description: Every year Jagannath rides out in a massive chariot pulled by thousands of devotees. The English word 'juggernaut' comes from this unstoppable chariot procession.

places.name: Badrinath
places.modernInfo: One of 4 Char Dham. Badri = jujube tree. Open May–November. Alaknanda river flows below.

scenes.title: Vishnu meditates under Badri tree
scenes.description: Vishnu meditated here under a Badri (jujube) tree for thousands of years. Lakshmi, his consort, took the form of the tree to protect him from the harsh Himalayan cold.
